description: CE migration: add nullable phase_id UUID column to project_status_mappings with FK to project_phases(phase_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
CE migration: add index on (tenant, project_id, phase_id) to project_status_mappings
CE migration: add nullable template_phase_id UUID column to project_template_status_mappings with FK to project_template_phases(template_phase_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
EE/Citus companion migration: FK constraints use composite keys (tenant, phase_id) and (tenant, template_phase_id) for distributed tables
Add phase_id?: string to IProjectStatusMapping interface in packages/types and server/src/interfaces
Add phase_id?: string to ProjectStatus type in packages/types and server/src/interfaces
Model: getProjectStatusMappings() accepts optional phaseId param and filters by phase_id
Model: new getEffectiveStatusMappings(knex, tenant, projectId, phaseId) returns phase statuses if configured, else project defaults (phase_id IS NULL)
Model: getProjectTaskStatuses() threads phaseId through to use effective resolution
Action: addStatusToProject() accepts optional phaseId and stores in mapping
Action: getProjectStatusMappings() accepts optional phaseId and filters accordingly
Action: reorderProjectStatuses() scopes reordering to phase if phaseId provided
New action: copyProjectStatusesToPhase(projectId, phaseId) copies project defaults as phase-specific mappings
New action: removePhaseStatuses(phaseId) deletes all phase-specific mappings, reverting to project defaults
Action: getProjectTaskStatuses() in projectActions.ts accepts optional phaseId and uses effective resolution
moveTaskToPhase(): same-project cross-phase moves resolve status — same name in target → keep it
moveTaskToPhase(): no name match + source was open → first open status by display_order in target phase
moveTaskToPhase(): no name match + source was closed → first closed status by display_order in target phase
moveTaskToPhase(): cross-project moves continue to work as before
Client portal: getClientProjectStatuses() accepts optional phaseId and uses effective resolution
ProjectDetail.tsx: fetch phase-effective statuses when selectedPhase changes instead of project-wide statuses
ProjectDetail.tsx: visibleKanbanStatuses, statusTaskCounts, and completedTasksCount use phase-effective statuses
KanbanBoard renders columns from phase-effective statuses passed via props (minimal change)
TaskStatusSelect shows effective statuses for the task's phase via props
ProjectTaskStatusSettings: phase selector dropdown/tabs above status list with 'Project Defaults' as first option
ProjectTaskStatusSettings: per-phase toggle 'Use project defaults' vs 'Custom statuses for this phase'
ProjectTaskStatusSettings: toggling to custom offers 'Copy from project defaults' action via copyProjectStatusesToPhase
ProjectTaskStatusSettings: toggling back to defaults shows confirmation dialog, then calls removePhaseStatuses
AddStatusDialog: accepts optional phaseId and passes it to addStatusToProject()
Client portal ProjectDetailView: fetch phase-effective statuses when phase selection changes
Client portal ClientKanbanBoard: renders phase-specific status columns from props
Client portal ClientTaskListView: groups tasks by phase-effective status groups
Template: project_template_status_mappings supports template_phase_id for per-phase template statuses
TemplateStatusManager: phase selector allows per-phase status configuration
TemplateStatusColumnsStep (wizard): supports phase-aware status setup
Project creation from template: copies phase-specific template status mappings to project_status_mappings with correct phase_id
Phase task CSV import: IImportReferenceData.statusMappings resolves against phase-effective statuses
Event payloads for PROJECT_STATUS_ADDED/UPDATED/DELETED/REORDERED include optional phaseId
ProjectTaskStatusEditor and ProjectTaskStatusSelector handle phase statuses when creating project from template
Backward compatibility: projects with no phase-specific statuses behave identically to before (all phases use project defaults)
